photos.desc: Raiders legends Laurie Daley, Bradley Clyde, and Mal Meninga celebrate the 36-12 Grand Final demolition of Canterbury.
photos.hint_context: A Grand Final win at a Sydney stadium

photos.desc: A view of the Sears Morton Sales store on Bunda Street, Civic, long before the Canberra Centre took over.
photos.hint_context: Old Civic shopfronts before the shopping centre era

photos.desc: The 'Down to Earth Confest' demonstration against uranium mining at Old Parliament House.
photos.hint_context: A protest outside a famous white building in the capital

photos.desc: Members of the Dutch carnival group 'The Windmills' downing steins at the Oktoberfest Parade.
photos.hint_context: An international cultural festival in Canberra

photos.desc: Crowds gathering at the newly built Capitol Theatre in Manuka.
photos.hint_context: An early entertainment venue in the new capital city

photos.desc: Women march through Civic's Petrie Plaza in a 'Reclaim the Night' march to demonstrate women's right to walk alone safely.
photos.hint_context: A social justice march through central Canberra

photos.desc: Ikea Country Manager David Hood, ACT Chief Minister Katy Gallagher, and Andrew Barr MLA announce the opening of the Canberra store.
photos.hint_context: A major retail announcement involving a Swedish furniture brand

photos.desc: HRH Queen Elizabeth visits the Australian War Memorial and lays a wreath at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier.
photos.hint_context: A royal visit to one of Canberra's most important institutions

photos.desc: The BP service station in Duffy on Burrinjuck Drive engulfed in flames during the devastating January bushfires.
photos.hint_context: Canberra's worst natural disaster in modern times
